regression.reason: Regression-E2E coverage delivered inside SCOPE-081-01 itself: scopes.md Test Plan row T-081-E1 designates ml/tests/integration/test_deadletter_parity.py::test_poison_message_publishes_to_deadletter_subject as the persistent live-stack regression-E2E protecting poison-pill routing to deadletter.<subject>; SCN-081-04 (test_failure_counts_attribute_removed) is the explicit regression for the leaky _failure_counts dict that this spec excises; flat DoD bullets D01-14 and the second regression-E2E bullet record the regression invariant. No separate regression phase is needed for a single-scope ship-parity follow-up whose substrate (spec 022 + spec 046) is already certified done.
regression.justification: This is a ship-parity follow-up that mirrors an existing, already-hardened Go pattern. The regression risk is contained to one file (ml/app/nats_client.py) and is protected by the live-stack integration test plus the unit-test asserting _failure_counts removal.

simplify.reason: N/A — spec 081 delivers new code that mirrors an existing, already-simplified Go pattern (publishSynthesisToDeadLetter in internal/pipeline/synthesis_subscriber.go). The implementation is the minimum to satisfy parity: single subscribe_all env read (no per-subject overrides, no re-reads), a single _handle_poison function, and a flat SUBJECT_TO_STREAM lookup table. There is no prior implementation to simplify and no over-engineering to remove.
simplify.justification: Spec 046 already executed the simplify phase on the substrate (NATS reconnect-contract + EnsureStreams minimal API). Spec 081 introduces no helpers, abstractions, or alternative paths.

stabilize.reason: N/A — the spec's purpose IS the stabilization (closing the parity gap surfaced by FOLLOWUP-046-PY-SIDECAR). There is no separate post-implement stabilize-sweep needed because the Python sidecar dead-letter path now mirrors the Go path that already passed spec 046's stabilize phase, and the integration test exercises the production code path end-to-end against the live test NATS stack.
stabilize.justification: Operational hardening of the JetStream consumer surface was the explicit scope of this spec. The bubbles.test live-integration run against the live stack closed the only stabilize-style concern (no in-memory _failure_counts growth, bounded redelivery, deadletter persistence).

security.reason: N/A — spec 081 introduces no new attack surface. The change set is limited to: (a) two integer SST keys (max_deliver, ack_wait_seconds) read fail-loud via os.environ[...], (b) ConsumerConfig threading through the existing pull_subscribe call, (c) replacement of the in-process _failure_counts dict with msg.metadata.num_delivered, (d) republishing the existing message payload (bytes-for-bytes preserved) to deadletter.<subject> with 6 audit headers. No new auth identity, no new network endpoint, no new file path, no PII exposure, no secrets handled, no deserialization of attacker-controlled data, no privilege boundary crossed. The dead-letter envelope contains only operational metadata (subject, stream, delivery count, RFC3339 timestamp, error string, consumer name) — no user data beyond what was already in the original message.
security.justification: Spec 046 executed the security phase on the substrate (auth_token bootstrap, no-defaults SST contract, fail-loud KeyError reads). Spec 081 only extends the same fail-loud SST pattern to two additional integer keys and reuses spec 046's connect() identity handshake without modification.

docs.reason: N/A — audit-phase scan of docs/Architecture.md, docs/Operations.md, docs/Testing.md, and docs/smackerel.md confirmed that the internal JetStream consumer dead-letter pattern (Go-side publishSynthesisToDeadLetter from spec 022/046, Python-side _handle_poison from spec 081) is NOT operator-facing documentation. The only 'dead-letter' references in docs/ are to the unrelated ntfy notification-source adapter (spec 054) operator UI/replay flow. There is no asymmetric documentation gap to close because the Go-side substrate is also not documented in docs/ — both live entirely within the runtime contract and are exercised through their respective test suites.
docs.justification: Evidence: grep -nE 'deadletter|dead-letter|num_delivered|max_deliver|NATS_CONSUMER_' across docs/**/*.md returns only ntfy-adapter matches; grep for 'sidecar.*nats' / 'ml.*nats' in docs/Architecture.md returns only the generic 'Go core + Python ML sidecar over NATS' topology paragraph (line 17-18) which is unchanged by this spec.

chaos.reason: N/A — the adversarial coverage required for the dead-letter / bounded-redelivery / fail-loud-SST surface was executed at the substrate level by spec 046 (certifiedCompletedPhases includes 'chaos'; 13 unbounded/mis-configured failure modes shown to fail loud at config-generate or startup). Spec 081 inherits that chaos coverage because it consumes the same SST contract and the same JetStream consumer plumbing. Spec 081's own adversarial cases (missing env vars, non-integer values, publish-before-term invariant violation) are already covered by unit tests test_subscribe_all_fails_loud_when_consumer_env_missing[×2], test_no_getenv_fallback_defaults_for_consumer_env, test_deadletter_publish_failure_results_in_nak_not_term, test_below_max_deliver_naks_without_publishing, and the D01-3 generator fail-loud adversarial probe (removed YAML key → exit 1 with named key).
chaos.justification: For a ship-parity follow-up that wires one additional language binding (Python) into an already-chaos-tested Go pattern, the adversarial cases that matter are the ones inherent to the new code path — and those are covered by the unit and integration tests in SCOPE-081-01. A separate chaos phase would duplicate work without surfacing new failure modes.

The canonical set is the Hard Constraint') so drift is documented, not silent.
